message:
feat: add claims expiry monitoring and enhance ack tracking with age/thread info

This commit adds proactive claims management with an expiry warning command,
enriches the acknowledgement tracking interface with thread context and age
information, and applies a minor Pythonic code improvement to file handling.

=============================================================================
CHANGES OVERVIEW
=============================================================================

Modified Files:
  • src/mcp_agent_mail/cli.py - New claims soon command, enhanced acks pending
  • src/mcp_agent_mail/storage.py - Pythonic file handling improvement

New Commands:
  • claims soon: Show claims expiring within timeframe (with countdown)

Enhanced Commands:
  • acks pending: Added Thread column and Ack Age column

Changes by Category:
  1. Claims Management: Proactive expiry monitoring
  2. Ack Tracking: Enhanced visibility (thread, age)
  3. Code Quality: Pathlib best practices

=============================================================================
DETAILED CHANGES
=============================================================================

1. NEW COMMAND: claims soon (cli.py)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Location: src/mcp_agent_mail/cli.py:252-313

PURPOSE:
--------
Proactive monitoring of claims nearing expiration to enable timely renewals
or coordination handoffs. Helps prevent claim expiration during active work.

SIGNATURE:
----------
  @claims_app.command("soon")
  def claims_soon(
      project: str = typer.Argument(...),
      minutes: int = typer.Option(30, min=1, help="Show claims expiring within N minutes"),
  ) -> None:
      """Show claims expiring soon to prompt renewals or coordination."""

PARAMETERS:
-----------
  project (positional, required):
    Project slug or human key
    Example: "backend" or "Backend API"

  --minutes (option, default: 30):
    Timeframe in minutes for "expiring soon"
    Minimum: 1 minute
    Example: --minutes 60 (show claims expiring within 1 hour)

IMPLEMENTATION:
---------------

Step 1: Query active claims
  stmt = (
      select(Claim, Agent.name)
      .join(Agent, Claim.agent_id == Agent.id)
      .where(
          Claim.project_id == project_record.id,
          cast(Any, Claim.released_ts).is_(None),
      )
      .order_by(asc(Claim.expires_ts))
  )

  Filters:
    • Only unreleased claims (released_ts IS NULL)
    • Ordered by expiry time (soonest first)

Step 2: Filter by cutoff time
  now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
  cutoff = now + timedelta(minutes=minutes)
  soon = [(c, a) for (c, a) in rows if c.expires_ts <= cutoff]

  Logic:
    • Calculate cutoff: current time + N minutes
    • Include claims expiring before cutoff
    • Includes already-expired claims (negative countdown)

Step 3: Display with countdown
  Helper function:
    def _fmt_delta(dt: datetime) -> str:
        delta = dt - now
        total = int(delta.total_seconds())
        sign = "-" if total < 0 else ""
        total = abs(total)
        h, r = divmod(total, 3600)
        m, s = divmod(r, 60)
        return f"{sign}{h:02d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}"

  Format examples:
    • Expires in 1h 15m 30s: "01:15:30"
    • Expires in 45s: "00:00:45"
    • Expired 30m ago: "-00:30:00"

OUTPUT:
-------
Rich table with countdown column:

  Claims expiring within 30m — Backend
  ┏━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━┓
  ┃ ID ┃ Agent   ┃ Pattern  ┃ Exclusive┃ Expires   ┃ In       ┃
  ┡━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━┩
  │ 42 │ BlueLake│ src/*.py │ yes      │ 2025-...  │ 00:15:30 │
  │ 35 │ RedForest│ docs/**  │ no       │ 2025-...  │ 00:28:45 │
  │ 31 │ BlueLake│ test/*.py│ yes      │ 2025-...  │ -00:05:20│
  └────┴─────────┴──────────┴──────────┴───────────┴──────────┘

Column details:
  • ID: Claim identifier
  • Agent: Claim holder
  • Pattern: Path pattern (glob)
  • Exclusive: yes/no (blocks other agents)
  • Expires: ISO timestamp (absolute time)
  • In: Countdown (relative time) ← NEW

Note: Negative countdown indicates already expired (red flag)

USAGE:
------
  # Default: claims expiring within 30 minutes
  mcp-mail claims soon backend

  # Custom timeframe: 1 hour
  mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 60

  # Very tight deadline: 5 minutes
  mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 5

USE CASES:
----------

A. Pre-work Check:
   Before starting a long task, check if claims will expire:

   $ mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 120

   If claims shown: Renew before starting work
   If no claims: Safe to proceed

B. Continuous Monitoring:
   Run periodically to monitor claim health:

   #!/bin/bash
   while true; do
       mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 15
       sleep 300  # Check every 5 minutes
   done

C. Automated Alerts:
   Integrate with notification system:

   #!/bin/bash
   OUTPUT=$(mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 10)
   if echo "$OUTPUT" | grep -q "│"; then
       send-notification "Claims expiring soon!"
   fi

D. Handoff Coordination:
   Team leader checks all agents' claims before shift change:

   $ mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 60

   Identifies claims needing renewal or handoff

COMPARISON TO claims list:
---------------------------
  claims list:
    • Shows ALL active claims (or all including released)
    • No time filtering
    • No countdown display
    • Use case: Full audit

  claims soon:
    • Shows only claims expiring within timeframe
    • Time-filtered (configurable)
    • Countdown display (In column)
    • Use case: Proactive monitoring

Both complement each other for comprehensive claim management.

BENEFITS:
---------
  ✓ Proactive: Catch expiring claims before they block work
  ✓ Flexible: Configurable timeframe (minutes option)
  ✓ Visual: Countdown shows urgency at a glance
  ✓ Historical: Shows expired claims (negative countdown)
  ✓ Actionable: Enables timely renewals or handoffs

EXPIRY WARNING WORKFLOW:
-------------------------
  1. Agent runs: mcp-mail claims soon backend
  2. Sees claim expiring in 10 minutes
  3. Options:
     a. Renew claim (if work continues)
     b. Release claim (if work complete)
     c. Coordinate handoff (if passing to another agent)
  4. Prevents claim expiration mid-task

2. ENHANCED COMMAND: acks pending (cli.py)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Location: src/mcp_agent_mail/cli.py:316-377

ENHANCEMENTS:
-------------
Added two new columns to the pending acknowledgements table:
  1. Thread: Shows thread_id for conversation context
  2. Ack Age: Shows how long ack has been pending

A. Thread Column:
   Purpose: Provide conversation context

   Implementation:
     table.add_column("Thread")
     # ...
     table.add_row(
         str(message.id),
         message.thread_id or "",  # ← NEW
         message.subject,
         # ...
     )

   Value: Thread identifier or empty string
   Use: Correlate with thread conversations

B. Ack Age Column:
   Purpose: Show SLA compliance / urgency

   Implementation:
     now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

     def _age(dt: datetime) -> str:
         delta = now - dt
         total = int(delta.total_seconds())
         h, r = divmod(max(total, 0), 3600)
         m, s = divmod(r, 60)
         return f"{h:02d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}"

     for message, read_ts, _ack_ts, kind in rows:
         age = _age(message.created_ts)
         table.add_row(..., age)

   Format: HH:MM:SS (hours:minutes:seconds)
   Examples:
     • "00:15:30" - Pending for 15 minutes 30 seconds
     • "02:45:00" - Pending for 2 hours 45 minutes
     • "25:10:15" - Pending for 25+ hours (SLA violation?)

OUTPUT BEFORE:
--------------
  Pending ACKs for BlueLake (Backend)
  ┏━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━┓
  ┃ Msg ID ┃ Subject        ┃ Kind ┃ Created    ┃ Read       ┃
  ┡━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━┩
  │ 42     │ Deploy approve │ to   │ 2025-10... │ 2025-10... │
  │ 35     │ Code review    │ cc   │ 2025-10... │            │
  └────────┴────────────────┴──────┴────────────┴────────────┘

OUTPUT AFTER:
-------------
  Pending ACKs for BlueLake (Backend)
  ┏━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━━━┳━━━━━━━━━━┓
  ┃ Msg ID ┃ Thread ┃ Subject        ┃ Kind ┃ Created    ┃ Read       ┃ Ack Age  ┃
  ┡━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━━━╇━━━━━━━━━━┩
  │ 42     │ 17     │ Deploy approve │ to   │ 2025-10... │ 2025-10... │ 02:30:15 │
  │ 35     │ 12     │ Code review    │ cc   │ 2025-10... │            │ 01:45:00 │
  └────────┴────────┴────────────────┴──────┴────────────┴────────────┴──────────┘

New columns provide:
  • Thread: Context for conversation tracking
  • Ack Age: SLA monitoring and urgency indicator

BENEFITS OF THREAD COLUMN:
---------------------------
  1. Context: See which conversation the ack belongs to
  2. Correlation: Cross-reference with git log --grep "Thread: X"
  3. Priority: Related acks in same thread can be batch-acknowledged
  4. Analytics: Track ack patterns per thread

BENEFITS OF ACK AGE COLUMN:
----------------------------
  1. Urgency: Immediate visual indicator of pending time
  2. SLA Monitoring: Identify violations (e.g., >4h policy)
  3. Prioritization: Sort by age (oldest = highest priority)
  4. Accountability: Track response time metrics

USE CASES WITH NEW COLUMNS:
----------------------------

A. Thread-based Triage:
   $ mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake

   See Thread column: Multiple acks from thread 17
   Action: Review entire thread, acknowledge all at once

B. SLA Enforcement:
   $ mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake

   See Ack Age: 25:30:00 (over 1 day old)
   Action: Escalate to manager or remind agent

C. Priority Sorting:
   Currently sorted by Created (newest first)
   Could enhance to sort by Ack Age (oldest first) for priority view

D. Reporting:
   Export to CSV for analysis:

   $ mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake | tee acks-report.txt

   Parse Ack Age column to calculate average response time

COMPARISON TO RESOURCE VIEW:
-----------------------------
  resource://views/ack-required/{agent}:
    • Returns JSON (programmatic)
    • Does not include age calculation
    • Does not include thread_id (could be added)

  mcp-mail acks pending:
    • Returns Rich table (human-readable)
    • Includes age calculation (HH:MM:SS)
    • Includes thread_id for context
    • Enhanced for operational use

IMPLEMENTATION DETAILS:
-----------------------

A. Thread retrieval:
   Message model already has thread_id field
   Simply display: message.thread_id or ""

B. Age calculation:
   Calculate delta from message creation to now
   Format as HH:MM:SS using divmod arithmetic
   Always non-negative (max with 0)

C. Helper function scope:
   _age() defined inside acks_pending() function
   Local scope, not reusable (could extract if needed elsewhere)

ALIGNMENT WITH TODO:
--------------------
From TODO.md:
  - [x] Offer CLI command to review ack status (cli list-acks)
    - [ ] In progress: Implemented; extend with ack age and thread columns

Status: NOW COMPLETE ✓
  • Ack age column: ✓ Implemented
  • Thread column: ✓ Implemented

This enhancement addresses the TODO item for extending ack tracking output.

3. CODE IMPROVEMENT: Pathlib file handling (storage.py)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

Location: src/mcp_agent_mail/storage.py:187

CHANGE:
-------
  BEFORE:
    with open(digest_path, mode, encoding="utf-8") as f:

  AFTER:
    with digest_path.open(mode, encoding="utf-8") as f:

RATIONALE:
----------
When working with pathlib.Path objects, using .open() is more Pythonic than
passing the path to open().

Benefits:
  ✓ Consistency: All file operations on Path objects use .open()
  ✓ Type safety: Path.open() is properly typed, open(Path) requires cast
  ✓ Readability: Clear that digest_path is a Path object
  ✓ Best practice: PEP 519 encourages Path methods over builtin functions

Context:
  Function: _update_thread_digest()
  Purpose: Append message summary to thread digest file
  File: messages/threads/{thread_id}.md

Impact:
  • Functionally identical (same behavior)
  • Improved code style
  • Better type inference

This follows Python pathlib best practices and aligns with modern code style.

=============================================================================
WORKFLOW EXAMPLES
=============================================================================

WORKFLOW 1: Proactive Claim Management
---------------------------------------
Agent about to start 2-hour task:

  1. Check claims expiring soon:
     $ mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 120

     Output shows: src/app.py claim expires in 01:30:00

  2. Decision: Renew claim before starting work
     (Use MCP tool or API to extend claim TTL)

  3. Verify renewal:
     $ mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 120

     Output shows: src/app.py claim expires in 03:30:00 ✓

WORKFLOW 2: SLA Monitoring for Acks
------------------------------------
Project manager daily review:

  1. Check all agents' pending acks:
     $ mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake
     $ mcp-mail acks pending backend RedForest
     $ mcp-mail acks pending backend GreenMountain

  2. Identify SLA violations (Ack Age > 04:00:00):
     BlueLake has ack pending for 06:30:15 (violation!)

  3. Escalate:
     Send reminder to BlueLake
     CC team lead if not acknowledged within 1 hour

  4. Track resolution:
     Re-run command to verify ack cleared

WORKFLOW 3: Thread-based Triage
--------------------------------
Agent reviews pending acks:

  1. List pending acks with thread context:
     $ mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake

     Output shows:
       Thread 17: 3 messages needing ack
       Thread 25: 1 message needing ack

  2. Review thread 17 conversation:
     $ git log --grep "Thread: 17"
     # Or use MCP resource: resource://thread/17

  3. Batch acknowledge:
     Review entire conversation
     Acknowledge all 3 messages at once

  4. Verify cleared:
     $ mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake

     Thread 17 messages should be gone

WORKFLOW 4: Automated Monitoring
---------------------------------
Cron job or daemon for continuous monitoring:

  ```bash
  #!/bin/bash
  # Check claims and acks every 10 minutes

  while true; do
      # Check claims expiring within 15 minutes
      CLAIMS=$(mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 15)
      if echo "$CLAIMS" | grep -q "│"; then
          send-alert "Claims expiring soon!" "$CLAIMS"
      fi

      # Check acks older than 2 hours
      ACKS=$(mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake)
      if echo "$ACKS" | grep -E "[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}" | grep -q "^[2-9]"; then
          send-alert "Old pending acks!" "$ACKS"
      fi

      sleep 600  # 10 minutes
  done
  ```

=============================================================================
TESTING VERIFICATION
=============================================================================

Manual Testing:
---------------

1. Test claims soon:
   # Create claim with short TTL (via MCP)
   $ mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 60

   Expected: Claim appears with countdown

   # Wait and re-run
   $ mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 60

   Expected: Countdown decreased

2. Test acks pending enhancements:
   # Send messages requiring ack (via MCP)
   $ mcp-mail acks pending backend BlueLake

   Expected:
     • Thread column shows thread IDs
     • Ack Age shows time since creation
     • Format is HH:MM:SS

3. Test time formatting:
   Create acks at different times:
     • Recent (5 min ago): Should show 00:05:XX
     • Old (2 hours ago): Should show 02:00:XX
     • Very old (1 day ago): Should show 24:00:XX+

Integration Tests Needed:
--------------------------
  • Test claims soon with various timeframes (1, 30, 60, 120 minutes)
  • Test with no expiring claims (empty table)
  • Test with expired claims (negative countdown) - verify handling
  • Test acks pending Thread column with threaded messages
  • Test acks pending Ack Age accuracy (create, wait, measure)

Edge Cases:
-----------
  • claims soon --minutes 0: Should error (min=1)
  • claims soon with no active claims: Empty table
  • acks pending with no thread_id: Empty string in Thread column
  • Very old acks (>100 hours): Format still HH:MM:SS (no overflow)

=============================================================================
ALIGNMENT WITH TODO AND ROADMAP
=============================================================================

TODO Items Completed:
---------------------
  ✓ CLI command to list active claims with expiry countdowns
    → Implemented: claims soon command

  ✓ Extend ack output with ack age and thread columns
    → Implemented: Thread and Ack Age columns in acks pending

TODO Items Progressed:
----------------------
  ⧗ Add CLI command to list active claims with expiry countdowns,
    and optionally raise warnings for soon-to-expire leases

    Status: Claims soon implemented, warnings could be enhanced:
      • Could add color coding (red for <5 min, yellow for <15 min)
      • Could add terminal bell for urgent expirations
      • Could add email/notification integration

Future Enhancements:
--------------------
From TODO, could add:

1. claims renew command:
   $ mcp-mail claims renew <claim_id> --extend 3600

   Extend claim TTL without releasing and re-claiming

2. acks summary command:
   $ mcp-mail acks summary backend

   Show aggregate stats:
     • Total pending acks per agent
     • Oldest ack age
     • Average ack age
     • SLA compliance percentage

3. Integration with notifications:
   $ mcp-mail claims soon backend --minutes 10 --notify

   Send desktop notification or email if claims found

=============================================================================
PERFORMANCE CONSIDERATIONS
=============================================================================

claims soon:
------------
  Query: Same as claims list (all active claims)
  Filtering: Client-side (in Python after query)

  Optimization opportunity:
    Could add WHERE clause to filter by expires_ts:

    .where(
        Claim.expires_ts <= cutoff,
        Claim.released_ts.is_(None)
    )

    Benefit: Database filters before Python processing
    Trade-off: More complex query construction

    Current approach: Simple and clear, negligible performance impact

acks pending:
-------------
  Query: Same as before (no performance change)

  New calculations:
    • Thread column: Direct field access (instant)
    • Age calculation: Simple arithmetic (microseconds)

  Impact: Negligible (< 1ms per row)

=============================================================================
BREAKING CHANGES
=============================================================================

None. All changes are additive:
  ✓ New command added (claims soon)
  ✓ New columns added (Thread, Ack Age)
  ✓ Existing commands unchanged
  ✓ Output format extended, not modified

Backward compatibility:
  • Scripts parsing acks pending output may need updates
    (new columns change column positions)
  • Recommendation: Parse by header names, not column index

=============================================================================
SUMMARY
=============================================================================

This commit enhances operational visibility for claims and acknowledgements:

New Features:
  • claims soon: Proactive expiry monitoring with countdown
  • acks pending enhancements: Thread context and age tracking
  • Pythonic improvement: Path.open() instead of open(Path)

Operational Benefits:
  ✓ Proactive claim management (prevent expirations)
  ✓ SLA monitoring (track ack response times)
  ✓ Thread context (conversation awareness)
  ✓ Visual urgency (countdown/age displays)

Files modified: 2
Lines added: ~75 (new command + enhancements)
TODO items completed: 2

Result: Enhanced CLI tooling for proactive operational monitoring,
SLA compliance tracking, and improved context for message workflows.

files.patch:
@@ -249,6 +249,69 @@ async def _run() -> tuple[Project, list[tuple[Claim, str]]]:
     console.print(table)
 
 
+@claims_app.command("soon")
+def claims_soon(
+    project: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Project slug or human key"),
+    minutes: int = typer.Option(30, min=1, help="Show claims expiring within N minutes"),
+) -> None:
+    """Show claims expiring soon to prompt renewals or coordination."""
+
+    async def _run() -> tuple[Project, list[tuple[Claim, str]]]:
+        project_record = await _get_project_record(project)
+        if project_record.id is None:
+            raise ValueError("Project must have an id")
+        await ensure_schema()
+        async with get_session() as session:
+            stmt = (
+                select(Claim, Agent.name)
+                .join(Agent, Claim.agent_id == Agent.id)
+                .where(
+                    Claim.project_id == project_record.id,
+                    cast(Any, Claim.released_ts).is_(None),
+                )
+                .order_by(asc(Claim.expires_ts))
+            )
+            rows = (await session.execute(stmt)).all()
+        return project_record, rows
+
+    try:
+        project_record, rows = asyncio.run(_run())
+    except ValueError as exc:
+        raise typer.BadParameter(str(exc)) from exc
+
+    from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta
+    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+    cutoff = now + timedelta(minutes=minutes)
+    soon = [(c, a) for (c, a) in rows if c.expires_ts <= cutoff]
+
+    table = Table(title=f"Claims expiring within {minutes}m — {project_record.human_key}", show_lines=False)
+    table.add_column("ID")
+    table.add_column("Agent")
+    table.add_column("Pattern")
+    table.add_column("Exclusive")
+    table.add_column("Expires")
+    table.add_column("In")
+
+    def _fmt_delta(dt: datetime) -> str:
+        delta = dt - now
+        total = int(delta.total_seconds())
+        sign = "-" if total < 0 else ""
+        total = abs(total)
+        h, r = divmod(total, 3600)
+        m, s = divmod(r, 60)
+        return f"{sign}{h:02d}:{m:02d}:{s:02d}"
+
+    for claim, agent_name in soon:
+        table.add_row(
+            str(claim.id),
+            agent_name,
+            claim.path_pattern,
+            "yes" if claim.exclusive else "no",
+            _iso(claim.expires_ts),
+            _fmt_delta(claim.expires_ts),
+        )
+    console.print(table)
+
 @acks_app.command("pending")
 def acks_pending(
     project: str = typer.Argument(..., help="Project slug or human key"),
@@ -286,17 +349,31 @@ async def _run() -> tuple[Project, Agent, list[tuple[Message, Any, Any, str]]]:
 
     table = Table(title=f"Pending ACKs for {agent_record.name} ({project_record.human_key})", show_lines=False)
     table.add_column("Msg ID")
+    table.add_column("Thread")
     table.add_column("Subject")
     table.add_column("Kind")
     table.add_column("Created")
     table.add_column("Read")
+    table.add_column("Ack Age")
+
+    now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
+    def _age(dt: datetime) -> str:
+        delta = now - dt
